items.title: Police killer Dezi Freeman had hundreds of child abuse files

children.content: Police killer Dezi Freeman was on the verge of having his secret stash of child abuse material discovered when he gunned down two officers outside his makeshift home.

gallery.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

thumbnail.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: Inflation expected to surge to highest level since 2023 driven by petrol prices

children.content: The ASX 200 has continued its downward spiral ahead of this morning's data release, which is likely to show inflation spiking at its highest level in two and a half years. Follow live.

image.altText: Petrol price

items.title: Former ASIO chief raised questions about assessment of Bondi gunman

children.content: While advising the royal commission examining the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, the former head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency questioned whether early assessments of gunman Naveed Akram were sufficiently rigorous.

image.altText: A man speaks into a mic.

items.title: King's speech to Congress hits on several Trump controversies

children.content: In a speech that was supposed to be apolitical, King Charles hits on some hot-button issues that are fuelling controversies around the Trump administration.

articles.title: Former FBI director charged over alleged social media photo threat to Trump

children.content: Former FBI director James Comey has been charged over a social media photo of seashells that officials say constituted a threat against US President Donald Trump.

image.altText: Comey gestures with both hands as he speaks on a stage.

articles.title: US regulator orders review of TV licence after Trump criticises Kimmel

children.content: It comes after both Donald and Melania Trump demanded the network cancel Kimmel's late-night comedy show over a joke they described as a call to violence, days before an alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

image.altText: Jimmy Kimmel speaks onstage during the Oscars show, he is dressed in a tuxedo

items.title: Parents slam 'weak' school response after 21 girls targeted in deepfake scandal

children.content: Two parents of girls at a private school have criticised the institution's response to a deepfake incident, saying they were encouraged not to tell their daughters they had been identified in material shared in a boys' group chat.

image.altText: A boy with bruised knuckles sits and looks at his mobile phone.

items.title: Don't assume El Niño warming will bring record heat

children.content: Australia's weather pattern is in a reset, and the weather events happening this month will act like the ingredients to determine the flavour of our climate for the rest of the year.

image.altText: A man in boots standing on dusty, dry land.

items.title: Increasingly desperate search for 5yo Sharon Granites continues for fourth day

children.content: Up to 130 volunteers are expected to join the increasingly desperate search for missing Alice Springs girl Sharon Granites today after the five-year-old's disappearance at the weekend.

articles.title: As search for missing 5yo Sharon continues, a clearer picture of that night emerges

children.content: Five-year-old Sharon Granites vanished from an Alice Springs town camp late on Saturday night, with police alleging she has been abducted. Here's what we know about the night she went missing.

image.altText: young girl in blue shirt sits in chair holding arms out front

articles.title: Criminal history of man police are searching for over missing 5yo girl revealed

children.content: A man being sought in connection with the alleged abduction of five-year-old Sharon Granites near Alice Springs has a long history of violence and prison time in the Northern Territory.

image.altText: A mugshot of an Aboriginal man, serious expression looking directly at camera, yellow / orange pigment behind brown eyes.

items.title: PM to announce $45 million to fast-track housing, energy projects

children.content: The prime minister will announce $45 million to help speed housing and energy project approvals. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese

items.title: 'Double-headed monster' set to push up prices at the check-out

children.content: Companies that use plastic packaging are facing price pressures and supply disruptions, which they warn will likely mean higher costs for consumers. The cost of food-grade resin out of Singapore, which is used in milk bottles, has increased 110 per cent since the start of the Middle East war.

image.altText: A close up of hand, light green and white check shirt, holding white pellets above a large drum with pellets.

items.title: US Marines board suspected Iran blockade runner, Israel hits Gaza, Lebanon

children.content: US President Donald Trump says Iranian leadership has told him they are in a "state of collapse" and are urging him to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as US marines board cargo ships in the region and Israeli strikes kill three in Lebanon. Follow live.

image.altText: A boy walks beside an enormous pile of rubble

items.title: Does the gas industry pay its fair share of tax?

children.content: Advocates are proposing a flat 25 per cent tax on gas exports, but representatives say the industry is being unfairly targeted.

image.altText: An aerial shot of the Gorgon gas project on the coast of Western Australia.

items.title: In signing Jarome Luai, Papua New Guinea's power has become real

children.content: Jarome Luai's decision to sign with Papua New Guinea is a showcase of the economic power behind the Chiefs and marks the start of the greatest rugby league gold rush since Super League.

articles.title: Wests Tigers star Luai confirms move to PNG Chiefs

children.content: Jarome Luai will leave Wests Tigers at the end of the 2027 season, announcing he will join the PNG Chiefs for their entry to the NRL.

items.title: AI will be 'the biggest challenge of all' for workplaces if it's not controlled

children.content: Australia risks repeating the mistakes it made with social media unless it moves quickly to regulate the spread of artificial intelligence in the workplace, according to a new report.

items.title: Family claims grandmother's death after weight-loss surgery preventable

children.content: Joanne Downie underwent an elective gastric bypass surgery so she could live a healthier life for her grandchildren, but instead she died six days later in a regional Queensland hospital.

children.content: South-East Queensland has been under-represented in 5% Deposit Scheme approvals, with prospective first-home buyers heading to Melbourne for its "considerable affordability" benefits.

items.title: Greens sound alarm after salmon farm pours tonnes of antibiotics into waterway

children.content: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

items.title: Outback puts on a show as tourism 'down to a trickle'

children.content: A potentially 'catastrophic' drop in traffic has outback residents concerned for the rest of the tourist season. Community members in remote Queensland have come together for the grand opening of a new outdoor theatre and a ribbon-cutting ceremony, declaring their town open for business.

items.title: The link between viral hepatitis and one of Australia's deadliest cancers

children.content: An alarming new report has found liver cancer deaths have risen from 1.6 deaths per 100,000 people in the 1980s to 5.1 per 100,000 in 2018–2022.

items.title: How self-employment can help autistic people avoid workplace challenges

children.content: After the National Autism Strategy identifies the potential of self-employment to provide meaningful work for people with autism, neurodivergent people who are already self-employed say it can help overcome workplace barriers.
